completed_tasks.name: Create backend venv, install packages, pre-download Whisper weights
Attempt FlashAttention 2 install and record the outcome
Write HTTPS probe server and HTTPS setup doc skeleton
Build llm_cancel.py and parser/idleness unit tests
Build whisper_bench.py, reference transcript, and unit tests
Build tts_ttfa.py, short reference transcript, and unit tests
Run the real Whisper benchmark on the builder recording
Run the real TTS TTFA benchmark on the builder recording

remaining_tasks.name: Verify trusted HTTPS from the actual Android device
Install a local LLM server on OMEN-PC and run the cancel probe against it
Resolve TTS engine budget miss: simulated vs native streaming, optional accent listening

blockers: Android browser verification still pending for 00-02 Task 2
No measured TTS engine clears the v1 budget on OMEN-PC: F5 is simulated-streaming at 530.3 ms TTFA, XTTS native streaming is 595.2 ms, and Qwen3 simulated streaming is 7527.3 ms

The current OMEN-PC Qwen3-TTS result is a no-FlashAttention baseline only: the probe records eager attention, flash-attn is not installed on OMEN-PC, and any later Qwen3 comparison must distinguish eager-baseline numbers from a future FA2-enabled rerun
Phase 00 still needs a policy call on whether simulated streaming is acceptable for the fallback engine; F5 is fastest but not a true incremental streamer
Local LLM server install still pending for 00-06 Task 2

decisions.decision: Use only OMEN-PC at 192.168.1.199 for Phase 0 host commands
decisions.rationale: The local Codex workstation is not the backend and produced misleading execution results

decisions.decision: Use the canonical SSH identity .local/phase0-ssh/rayme_omen_phase0_ed25519 and bootstrap ~/.ssh from it each session
decisions.rationale: Persistent key material must not live in /tmp or be assumed durable under /home/agent/.ssh in containme; the repo bind mount is the verified persistent path

decisions.decision: Write repo artifacts locally but validate backend-only behavior over SSH on OMEN-PC
decisions.rationale: The git checkout is local Linux while the executable Windows CUDA environment lives on OMEN-PC

decisions.decision: Treat 00-02 Task 2 as builder-blocked until an actual Android browser verification is performed
decisions.rationale: The code and setup skeleton are ready, but the acceptance check requires the builder's actual Android phone and certificate trust flow

decisions.decision: Persist the voice benchmark fixtures in git before asking for recordings
decisions.rationale: The exact transcript text to read must live in repo state, not in transient chat memory

decisions.decision: Downgrade ctranslate2 to 3.24.0 on OMEN-PC while Phase 00 uses the CUDA 11.8 Torch stack
decisions.rationale: Official faster-whisper guidance says current CTranslate2 wheels need CUDA 12, and the CUDA 11 workaround is ctranslate2 3.24.0

decisions.decision: User explicitly approved the Coqui XTTS CPML on 2026-04-22 and OMEN-PC now persists that state via tos_agreed.txt
decisions.rationale: The license prompt required an explicit human choice; after approval, the accepted state was persisted on OMEN-PC so future XTTS runs no longer hit EOF at the prompt

decisions.decision: Classify XTTS as the only native streaming engine in the current Phase 00 benchmark; treat F5 and Qwen3 as simulated-streaming only
decisions.rationale: The updated probe now measures XTTS through its internal inference_stream path, while F5 only slices generated audio into chunks after synthesis and Qwen3 only simulates streaming text input

decisions.decision: Use F5-TTS as the primary cloned-voice path and keep XTTS as a compatibility-only option
decisions.rationale: User listening tests found XTTS clone quality very poor compared with F5. XTTS remains useful only as a compatibility fallback because it has native streaming support.

decisions.decision: Use the production-style F5 preset with short ack plus chunked remainder at speed 1.5 as the current responsiveness baseline
decisions.rationale: On OMEN-PC this profile delivered 528.4 ms ack TTFA, 1.259 s ack duration, 0 ms dead air after the ack, and a 69.36 s long-form combined render that sounds materially less sluggish than speed 1.0.

decisions.decision: Treat the current OMEN-PC Qwen3-TTS benchmark as an eager-attention baseline, not a FlashAttention-optimized result
decisions.rationale: The benchmark JSON on OMEN-PC records Qwen3 with flash_attention=eager, qwen_tts warns that flash-attn is not installed, and the separate FA2 install task previously failed on OMEN-PC. Any future Qwen3 performance comparison must call out whether FA2 is still absent or has been enabled.

next_action: Run ./scripts/bootstrap-rayme-ssh.sh restore, then plan and execute 00-07.1-attention-backend-matrix so Phase 00 has backend-labeled TTS measurements (`eager` / `sdpa` / `flash_attention_2` / `not_applicable`) before final writeback. Keep the current F5 production path as the leading candidate, XTTS as compatibility fallback, and treat existing OMEN-PC Qwen3 numbers as the no-FA2 eager baseline until a separately labeled rerun exists.
context_notes: SEV-1 process failure and user-trust incident. The voice benchmark fixtures are persisted in commit f207093, the live STT/TTS runs have now been executed on OMEN-PC, and the handoff must remember the CTranslate2 CUDA 11 workaround, the F5 probe shims that fixed the Windows crash, the current production-style F5 baseline at speed 1.5, the XTTS native-streaming result after explicit user-approved CPML acceptance, the fact that OMEN-PC Qwen3 numbers are an eager-attention no-FlashAttention baseline rather than an FA2-optimized result, the fact that containme does not persist /home/agent/.ssh across sessions, and the fact that git safe.directory may need to be re-added in fresh sessions and should be fixed automatically rather than treated as a blocker.

incident.summary: Phase 0 execution was attempted on the wrong machine, persistent SSH state was lost by using /tmp, and the user was repeatedly forced into manual recovery loops.
incident.user_impact: Multiple days of setup churn, repeated copy/paste, invalid or overlong instructions, and justified extreme frustration.
root_causes: Wrong execution host was used despite research pointing at OMEN-PC
Persistent SSH material was treated as temporary
Remote success was claimed before enough command evidence was gathered
Recovery loops were longer and more speculative than necessary
prevention_rules: Use OMEN-PC only for Phase 0 backend work
Never store persistent SSH material in /tmp or assume /home/agent/.ssh persists
If git reports dubious ownership for /d/Pedro/Repos/Program/RayMe, immediately run git config --global --add safe.directory /d/Pedro/Repos/Program/RayMe and continue
Verify hostname, whoami, python --version, and nvidia-smi before claiming backend access works
Prefer the shortest validated recovery path under user frustration

qwen3.interpretation: No-FlashAttention baseline on OMEN-PC. Qwen3 numbers here should not be treated as FA2-optimized.
